zone_summary: Notebook-related failures now carry curated error-code-specific messages/titles, ETag conflicts are remapped to a dedicated 422 notebook error, parsing/validation failures are funneled through NotebookException, and RunDag error mapping now preserves specific notebook codes or wraps generic notebook exceptions via a documented two-tier contract.

behavioral_changes.summary: Notebook API 412 ETag conflicts now surface as a coded notebook exception with HTTP 422 semantics, an MLV_NB_ETAG_CHANGED detail template, and the ProblemDetails title "Notebook recently updated".

Magic-command parsing failures now produce a user-facing MLV_MAGIC_COMMAND_NOT_SUPPORTED message that includes cell index, line number, and notebook id, and they flow through the explicit-error-code notebook path.

Multiple materialized-view definitions in one notebook cell now produce a cell-specific MLV_MULTIPLE_DEFINITION_CONFLICT_SINGLE_CELL detail and use the ProblemDetails title "Notebook validation error".
Notebook exceptions without an explicit ErrorCode are now wrapped in the MLV_LINEAGE_CREATION_NOTEBOOK_EXCEPTION template instead of returning raw exception text directly.
When a generic notebook exception has no NotebookId, the wrapped notebook-exception message now substitutes "Unknown" for the notebook placeholder.
When a generic notebook exception has no WorkspaceId, the wrapped notebook-exception message now substitutes "Unknown" for the workspace placeholder.
When a generic notebook exception has an empty/raw missing Message, the wrapper now inserts an empty string instead of propagating null/blank content into the user-facing detail.

RunDag notebook error mapping now follows a two-tier ErrorCode contract: coded notebook exceptions pass through their specific code/message, uncoded notebook exceptions are wrapped, and the returned text is formatted as `[ErrorCode]: ... failureType: ...`.

grounding_evidence.reason: This is the source of the ETag-specific remap from Notebook API 412 to a 422 notebook exception with an explicit error code.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: { ErrorCode.MLV_NB_ETAG_CHANGED, $"MLV_NB_ETAG_CHANGED: Contents in the Notebook {{{Constants.NotebookDetailsKey}}} changed after the operation started. Refresh and try again." },
grounding_evidence.reason: Defines the user-visible detail string for the new ETag notebook error.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: public const string NotebookRecentlyUpdatedTitle = "Notebook recently updated";
grounding_evidence.reason: Defines the new ProblemDetails title used for notebook ETag conflicts.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: ErrorCode.MLV_NB_ETAG_CHANGED => Constants.NotebookRecentlyUpdatedTitle,
grounding_evidence.reason: Maps the ETag notebook error code to the user-facing title.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: Title = title,
grounding_evidence.reason: Makes ProblemDetails emit the caller-selected title instead of always using the old fixed default.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: { ErrorCode.MLV_MAGIC_COMMAND_NOT_SUPPORTED, $"MLV_MAGIC_COMMAND_NOT_SUPPORTED: Magic commands (e.g., %pip, %run) in cell {{{Constants.CellIndexKey}}} at line {{{Constants.LineNumberKey}}} of Notebook '{{{Constants.NotebookIdKey}}}' are not supported in materialized lake view definitions. Remove or comment out magic commands and retry." },
grounding_evidence.reason: Defines the new placeholder-rich user-facing detail for magic-command parsing failures.
Populates the new magic-command message with cell/line/notebook values and rethrows it as a notebook exception carrying parsing context.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: : this(message, HttpStatusCode.UnprocessableEntity, workspaceId, notebookId, ErrorMapping.ErrorCode.MLV_MAGIC_COMMAND_NOT_SUPPORTED)
grounding_evidence.reason: The parsing-context constructor stamps magic-command failures with an explicit notebook error code, enabling pass-through handling downstream.
Controller behavior now passes through preformatted coded notebook errors and pairs them with an error-code-specific title.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: { ErrorCode.MLV_MULTIPLE_DEFINITION_CONFLICT_SINGLE_CELL, $"MLV_MULTIPLE_DEFINITION_CONFLICT_SINGLE_CELL: More than one materialized lake view definition exists in cell {{{Constants.CellIndexKey}}} of Notebook '{{{Constants.NotebookIdKey}}}'. Ensure only one view per cell." },
grounding_evidence.reason: Defines the new detail text for the single-cell multiple-definition validation failure.
This is where the controller-visible multiple-definition error becomes cell-specific and gains an explicit error code.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: public const string NotebookValidationErrorTitle = "Notebook validation error";
grounding_evidence.reason: Defines the new ProblemDetails title for notebook validation failures.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: ErrorCode.MLV_MULTIPLE_DEFINITION_CONFLICT_SINGLE_CELL => Constants.NotebookValidationErrorTitle,
grounding_evidence.reason: Maps the multiple-definition notebook error code to the notebook-validation ProblemDetails title.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: { ErrorCode.MLV_LINEAGE_CREATION_NOTEBOOK_EXCEPTION, $"MLV_LINEAGE_CREATION_NOTEBOOK_EXCEPTION: Failed to create lineage due to Notebook exception in notebook '{{{Constants.NotebookIdKey}}}' (workspace: '{{{Constants.WorkspaceIdKey}}}'). {{{Constants.ErrorMessageKey}}}Review Notebook logs and retry." },
grounding_evidence.reason: Defines the new wrapper template for generic notebook exceptions.

grounding_evidence.reason: Adds a null fallback for NotebookId when building the generic notebook exception message.

grounding_evidence.reason: Adds a null fallback for WorkspaceId when building the generic notebook exception message.

grounding_evidence.reason: Guards the wrapper against empty/raw missing exception text when constructing the generic notebook detail.
This added remarks block is a contract comment describing the branching rule downstream code must honor.
Implements the pass-through branch for notebook exceptions that already carry a specific user-facing error code/message.
Implements the uncoded-exception wrapper path and strips the registry prefix before RunDag reformats the message.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: var errorMessage = $"[{errorCode}]: {cleanMessage} failureType: {failureType}";
grounding_evidence.reason: Defines the new RunDag notebook error output format with resolved code and failureType suffix.
This branch is the controller-side wrapper for generic notebook exceptions that lack a specific ErrorCode.

scenario_sketches.title: ETag mismatch returns 422 notebook-recently-updated problem details
scenario_sketches.rationale: Drive the Notebook API path with a 412 PreconditionFailed response and assert the surfaced error uses 422 semantics, the MLV_NB_ETAG_CHANGED detail template, and the custom title "Notebook recently updated".

scenario_sketches.title: Magic command parse failures mention cell, line, and notebook id
scenario_sketches.rationale: Feed notebook content containing a `%` magic command and verify the resulting notebook error detail contains the MLV_MAGIC_COMMAND_NOT_SUPPORTED prefix plus the resolved cell index, line number, and notebook identifier.

scenario_sketches.title: Multiple MLV definitions in one cell return notebook-validation title and cell-specific detail
scenario_sketches.rationale: Build a notebook cell with more than one MLV definition and assert the surfaced response uses the single-cell conflict template and the "Notebook validation error" ProblemDetails title.

scenario_sketches.title: Generic notebook exceptions are wrapped with notebook and workspace context
scenario_sketches.rationale: Throw a NotebookException without ErrorCode but with notebook/workspace ids and a raw message, then verify the returned detail is the MLV_LINEAGE_CREATION_NOTEBOOK_EXCEPTION template rather than the raw exception string.

scenario_sketches.title: Missing notebook id falls back to 'Unknown' in wrapped notebook errors
scenario_sketches.rationale: Use an uncoded NotebookException whose NotebookId is null and verify the generic notebook wrapper substitutes `Unknown` in the notebook slot instead of emitting an empty/null identifier.

scenario_sketches.title: Missing workspace id falls back to 'Unknown' in wrapped notebook errors
scenario_sketches.rationale: Use an uncoded NotebookException whose WorkspaceId is null and verify the generic notebook wrapper substitutes `Unknown` in the workspace slot.

scenario_sketches.title: Empty notebook exception text does not inject blank raw-message content into the wrapper
scenario_sketches.rationale: Use an uncoded NotebookException with an empty Message and verify the wrapped detail omits raw-message insertion rather than surfacing `null`, duplicate spacing, or an extra fragment before `Review Notebook logs and retry.`

scenario_sketches.title: RunDag notebook error formatting keys off ErrorCode presence, not message text
scenario_sketches.rationale: Exercise MapExceptionToErrorInfo with two rows: one NotebookException carrying ErrorCode (must preserve its specific message/code) and one without ErrorCode (must wrap via MLV_LINEAGE_CREATION_NOTEBOOK_EXCEPTION), and assert both rows emit the `[code]: ... failureType: ...` format described by the new contract comment.
